_design_note: Orchestrator agent for a cloud ops team. This agent does NOT take direct infrastructure actions. Its role is to read state, coordinate, and delegate to specialist sub-agents. Each sub-agent has its own consent graph that governs what it can actually do. The orchestrator's direct action surface is intentionally narrow.

metadata.description: Cloud ops orchestrator. Reads infra state and delegates to specialist agents. No direct destructive capability.

infra_state._design_note: Read-only access to aggregate infrastructure state. Safe to do autonomously -- this is how the orchestrator decides what to delegate.

notifications._design_note: Orchestrator sends status updates and escalations autonomously. No channel management -- that's human-owned config.

delegation._design_note: This is the orchestrator's delegation manifest. Each trusted_agent entry defines a specialist sub-agent and the domains the orchestrator may invoke on its behalf. max_delegation_depth=1 prevents chain delegation: the orchestrator can delegate to sub-agents, but sub-agents cannot re-delegate further.

trusted_agents._design_note: ECS deployment agent handles all production deployment ops. Requires human approval before the orchestrator can invoke any ECS deployment action -- high blast radius.
Cost optimizer agent. Read and recommend actions are safe to delegate autonomously. The agent's own graph governs what it can execute.
Security scanner initiates scans autonomously. It cannot modify findings or policies -- its own graph enforces that. Orchestrator delegates scan initiation without human gate.
